task_objective: Implement watchdog safeties for suspended game processes and simplify synchronous threading executor wrapping via asyncio.to_thread, including remote sync safeguards
design_decisions: Changed self._paused_pids to a dict[int, float] to track pause epoch timestamps.

Designed a resource-friendly watchdog thread: lazily started on the first SIGSTOP, running a check every second, and automatically breaking out of its loop and terminating when no PIDs remain in the paused map. This ensures zero CPU usage when idle and prevents thread leaks in tests.

Bypassed watchdog auto-resumption when self._operation.is_running is True. This protects slow remote operations (like Ludusavi backing up to Google Drive via rclone/rsync) from being cut short and ensures the game is not resumed until the sync lock is released.
Integrated self._backend.stop() on plugin unload to gracefully shut down the watchdog and clean up any remaining paused processes.
Simplified _run_blocking in main.py to asyncio.shield(asyncio.to_thread(callback)) to leverage standard library threading pools, context propagation, and event loop safe returns.
results: All quality control checks (ruff, format, type-check) and all 248 tests pass successfully.
